title: Module Check: Windows Event Log Analysis

questions.question: What do the LogonType values in Event 4624 indicate? List at least three.
questions.answer: LogonType 2: Interactive (local console). LogonType 3: Network (e.g., SMB, WMI). LogonType 10: RemoteInteractive (RDP). LogonType 5: Service logon. LogonType 7: Unlock. Type 3 is most common for lateral movement; Type 10 for RDP-based attacks.

questions.question: How does Sysmon Event 10 (ProcessAccess) detect LSASS memory dumping?
questions.answer: Event 10 fires when one process opens a handle to another process's memory. Credential dumpers (Mimikatz, procdump) open LSASS with PROCESS_VM_READ access. Filter for: TargetImage = lsass.exe, GrantedAccess = 0x1010 or 0x1410 (VM read permissions). This detects Mimikatz and most LSASS dump tools.

questions.question: What is the DCSync attack and which Windows events detect it?
questions.answer: DCSync abuses the MS-DRSR (Directory Replication Service Remote) protocol to request password hashes from a DC as if the attacker were another DC — without needing to run code on the DC. Detection: Event 4662 on the DC (DsGetNCChanges — object access with GUID 1131f6aa...) and Sysmon 3 (outbound replication port 135/3268 from non-DC).

questions.question: Why is ProcessGUID a better correlation field than PID in Sysmon logs?

options.A: PIDs are randomised by the OS to prevent tracking
options.B: PIDs are reused after process termination; ProcessGUID is globally unique for the process lifetime
options.C: ProcessGUID includes the parent process, making tree reconstruction easier
options.D: PIDs are not logged in Sysmon

questions.explanation: Windows reuses PIDs shortly after a process exits. If a malicious process exits and a benign process gets the same PID, correlating events by PID would falsely link them. ProcessGUID is a UUID assigned at process creation and never reused — allowing reliable cross-event correlation throughout the process lifetime.

questions.question: In the PsExec attack kill chain, which four event sources provide detection coverage?
questions.explanation: PsExec kill chain: Sysmon 3 (lateral SMB connection), Event 4624 LogonType 3 (network authentication), Event 7045 (PSEXESVC service installation), Sysmon 1 (cmd.exe/payload process spawned by PSEXESVC). These four events across two log sources cover the full chain.
